hero.headline: Shoolini Online MBA in IT Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme with Pay After Placement and NAAC A+ accreditation

sections.tldr: Shoolini Online MBA in IT Management covers IT project management, enterprise systems, cybersecurity basics, digital transformation, software product management, and IT governance. PAP option Rs 1,50,000 or opt-out Rs 1,30,000. LinkedIn Learning access. KPMG certifications. NAAC A+. NIRF #69 University.

about.body:
IT Management is an increasingly critical MBA specialisation as organisations require professionals who bridge technical IT functions with business strategy. IT Managers, Product Managers, and Technology Directors are in demand across IT services, BFSI, manufacturing, healthcare, and e-commerce.

Shoolini University Online's MBA in IT Management covers enterprise systems and ERP management, IT project management and agile delivery, cybersecurity and information security management, digital transformation strategy, software product lifecycle management, IT governance and compliance, and IT infrastructure management. The programme is designed for IT professionals transitioning from technical to management roles, and for business professionals in tech-heavy environments.

Shoolini University holds NIRF #69 (University, 2025) and NAAC A+ accreditation. The LinkedIn Learning bundle is particularly well-matched to IT Management students — the platform provides current certifications in project management tools (JIRA, Trello, Asana), cloud platforms (AWS, Azure basics), cybersecurity awareness, and digital transformation frameworks alongside the academic curriculum.

The Pay After Placement option on Track 1 is available. EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 subjects are shared across all Shoolini MBA specialisations. Sem 3-4 below are specific to the IT Management specialisation.

subjects.name: Legal Aspects of Business
subjects.desc: Business contracts, corporate governance, consumer protection obligations, and regulatory compliance frameworks for managers.

subjects.name: Database Management System
subjects.desc: Relational database design, SQL basics for managers, data warehouse overview, and enterprise data governance frameworks.

subjects.name: Python for Business
subjects.desc: Python scripting for data analysis, automation basics, and business reporting workflows using Python.

subjects.name: Storytelling with Data
subjects.desc: Visual communication of data findings, chart selection, and translating analytics into business narratives for non-technical audiences.

subjects.name: Acing Interviews through AI
subjects.desc: AI-assisted mock interview practice, personal brand communication, and job-search strategy frameworks for management graduates.

subjects.name: Intro to Editing
subjects.desc: Professional editing skills for business documents, reports, presentations, and digital content.

subjects.name: Future Leader's Program
subjects.desc: Executive presence building, stakeholder influence, high-performance team leadership, and personal effectiveness frameworks for emerging managers.

subjects.name: Data Visualization
subjects.desc: Power BI and Tableau dashboard design, KPI metric selection, and visual data storytelling for executive and business audiences.

subjects.name: Cyber Security
subjects.desc: Information security management, ISO 27001 basics, incident response process, and GDPR and IT Act compliance for IT managers.

subjects.name: Data Ethics and Privacy
subjects.desc: Ethical data use frameworks, privacy by design principles, AI bias management, and responsible analytics governance.

subjects.name: Capstone Project
subjects.desc: Applied industry project integrating specialisation knowledge with structured problem-solving for a real-world business challenge.

subjects.name: Critical Thinking and Problem Solving
subjects.desc: Structured analytical frameworks for managerial decision-making, root-cause analysis, and complex business problem resolution.

whoHires.body:
IT Management MBA graduates from Shoolini University Online are hired across India's technology and tech-enabled sectors:

**Industries:** IT services, BFSI technology divisions, e-commerce tech teams, consulting firms, manufacturing IT departments, healthcare IT, and edtech

**Entry-level roles:** IT Project Coordinator, Business Analyst, Technology Manager (junior), ERP Consultant (junior), Product Analyst, Systems Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** IT Project Manager, Product Manager, Technology Manager, ERP Implementation Manager, Digital Transformation Lead, IT Head (SME)

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Technology, Chief Information Officer, Chief Technology Officer, Head of Digital, Director of IT, Chief Digital Officer

**Top hiring organisations:** TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL, Tech Mahindra, Capgemini, Accenture, Cognizant, IBM India, HDFC Bank technology, ICICI Bank tech, Bajaj Finserv tech, Amazon India tech, Flipkart engineering, and mid-market IT services companies across Chandigarh, Mohali, and North India IT clusters

skills.body:
The IT Management specialisation builds skills that IT services, BFSI tech, and product companies test at manager and analyst levels:

**Technical skills:**
- IT Project Management: project charter writing, WBS creation, Agile sprint planning, JIRA board management, and project health reporting
- Cybersecurity: ISO 27001 basics, data classification, access control policy writing, incident response process, and GDPR/IT Act compliance
- Enterprise Systems: ERP implementation lifecycle, change management for ERP rollouts, system integration basics, and post-go-live support management
- IT Governance: COBIT framework basics, IT audit readiness, vendor risk management, and SLA design for IT services

**Applied skills:**
- Product Management: PRD writing, user story mapping, product backlog prioritisation, and roadmap communication to engineering and business stakeholders
- Digital Transformation: current-state process mapping, technology selection framework, change management playbook, and digital maturity assessment

**Soft skills:** Translating technical constraints to non-technical business stakeholders, managing developer and vendor teams on deadline-driven projects, presenting IT investment cases to CFOs and boards, and facilitating technology adoption training for non-technical end users.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Shoolini Online MBA IT Management against peer programmes:

**vs CU Online MBA Information Technology:** CU at Rs 1,65,000 Tier 1 offers NIRF #19, PwC co-teaching, Harvard certificate, and a comprehensive IT curriculum. For IT professionals targeting consulting and advisory roles at large enterprises where the PwC credential and NIRF #19 brand carry weight, CU is the stronger choice. Shoolini at Rs 1,30,000 opt-out is 21% cheaper with NAAC A+ and adds the LinkedIn Learning technology certification bundle — particularly well-matched for IT professionals who want platform-specific skills alongside the management degree.

**vs LPU Online MBA Information Technology:** LPU has NAAC A++ and strong campus placement connections in IT sector. Fee approximately Rs 1,50,000-1,80,000. Shoolini at Rs 1,30,000 is comparably or more affordably priced and adds PAP. For IT professionals in North India's growing tech corridor (Chandigarh-Mohali), both are viable — Shoolini's LinkedIn Learning bundle gives an edge for self-paced skill development.

**vs Amity Online MBA Information Technology Management:** Amity at Rs 2,25,000 with NIRF #22 carries the strongest IT management brand in India's online MBA space. For large IT services firms where Amity brand is specifically a hiring filter, it justifies the premium. Shoolini at Rs 1,30,000 is 42% cheaper with NAAC A+ and PAP.

fees.body:
The Shoolini University Online MBA in IT Management carries a regular fee of Rs 2,00,000 total across 4 semesters (2-year programme). A Rs 500 one-time application fee applies. No registration fee, no exam fees, no project fees beyond the programme fee.

**Two offer tracks**:

**Offer 1 - MBA with Pay After Placement (PAP)**:
- Rs 50,000 scholarship applied. Effective fee Rs 1,50,000 (25% savings).
- Payment split: Rs 1,20,000 before placement (Rs 30,000 x 4 semesters) + Rs 30,000 payable after confirmed placement.

**Offer 2 - MBA Opt-out from PAP**:
- Rs 70,000 scholarship applied. Effective fee Rs 1,30,000 (35% savings).
- Payment: Rs 32,500 x 4 semesters.

**Merit scholarship (10% additional)**:
For candidates scoring 90% or above in Class 12 (UG entry) or 90% or above in UG degree. Non-combinable with PAP or opt-out tracks, stackable with lump-sum and annual discounts.

**Additional payment discounts (stackable with merit only)**:
- 10% off on full course fee with single lump-sum payment
- Additional 5% off on annual payment in full

**Zero-cost EMI**: Available directly through Shoolini University (not via external marketing partners or NBFCs). Contact our counsellor for current tenure options.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Shoolini each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive a Shoolini University MBA degree certificate with IT Management noted on the marksheet, issued under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards. NIRF #69 University and NAAC A+ carry recognition across India's IT services, BFSI technology, and digital employer base.

LinkedIn Learning certificates earned alongside the programme are shareable on LinkedIn and recognised by technology employers as evidence of current skill development. DigiLocker verification is available for the degree.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Shoolini University Online MBA IT Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Arun T.
items.liked: LinkedIn Learning cloud and project management certifications were directly applicable to my IT manager role
items.disliked: AI and machine learning for IT managers was covered briefly - needed more depth given how rapidly this area is evolving
items.body: I manage IT projects at an IT services company in Mohali. The combination of IT project management, agile delivery, and IT governance subjects gave me a structured management foundation to complement my technical background. LinkedIn Learning AWS basics and JIRA certification helped me lead cloud migration conversations with clients. PAP structure let me invest in the programme without large upfront risk. NAAC A+ from Shoolini cleared my company's MBA requirement for the Project Manager designation.

items.name: Kavita R.
items.liked: Cybersecurity governance and ISO 27001 basics were directly applicable to my compliance role
items.disliked: ERP content was generic - more SAP-specific depth would have helped given SAP's dominance in enterprise IT
items.body: I work in IT governance and compliance at a BFSI company. The cybersecurity management, GDPR and IT Act compliance, and IT audit readiness subjects were directly relevant. The MBA credential from a NAAC A+ university cleared the manager designation filter at my company. LinkedIn Learning cybersecurity courses supplemented the academic content with practical tool-level knowledge. Opted for Rs 1,30,000 track with zero-cost EMI.

items.name: Vikash S.
items.liked: Product management content was practical for transitioning from engineering to PM role
items.disliked: Placement support for Delhi NCR tech market was limited compared to Punjab and HP clusters where Shoolini has stronger networks
items.body: I transitioned from a software developer to product management using this MBA as the credential for the role change. PRD writing, user story mapping, and roadmap communication subjects were directly applicable. KPMG certification helped in client-facing product demos. The Rs 1,30,000 fee was competitive compared to bootcamps that cost more and don't give a recognised management degree. PAP option was available but I had a PM offer lined up.

faqs.question: Why is LinkedIn Learning particularly valuable for IT Management MBA students?
faqs.answer: LinkedIn Learning provides current courses on JIRA, Agile frameworks, AWS basics, cybersecurity awareness, and product management tools — skills that evolve faster than academic curricula can update. IT Management students can earn platform-recognised certifications in project management and cloud tools alongside the academic degree, making their profiles more immediately competitive with both traditional MBA and bootcamp graduates.

faqs.question: Does this MBA help with a transition from technical IT to management roles?
faqs.answer: Yes. The IT Management specialisation is specifically designed for technical professionals moving into management. IT project management, product lifecycle, digital transformation strategy, and IT governance subjects provide the business management frameworks that technical professionals typically lack. The management core from Sem 1-2 (Organizational Behaviour, Financial Management, Managerial Economics) builds cross-functional management literacy.

faqs.question: Is Shoolini IT Management MBA cheaper than CU and LPU?
faqs.answer: At Rs 1,30,000 opt-out, Shoolini is 21% cheaper than CU Tier 1 (Rs 1,65,000) and comparable to LPU. Shoolini uniquely offers Pay After Placement for IT candidates who want to defer part of the fee until after their post-MBA role is confirmed. CU adds PwC content and Harvard certificate; Shoolini adds LinkedIn Learning technology bundle and PAP.

faqs.question: What cybersecurity content is covered in IT Management?
faqs.answer: The IT Management specialisation covers ISO 27001 information security management basics, data classification and access control policy design, incident response process management, GDPR compliance requirements, and India's IT Act provisions relevant to data handling. These subjects are applicable for IT governance, compliance officer, and information security manager roles in BFSI and IT services companies.

faqs.question: Is the Shoolini IT Management MBA valid for IT project manager roles at large IT services companies?
faqs.answer: Yes. The UGC-DEB entitled, NAAC A+ degree satisfies management degree requirements at IT services companies for Project Manager and Senior Business Analyst designations. The IT Management specialisation's project management content covers agile, JIRA, and project health reporting frameworks that IT services companies test in internal promotion and lateral hire interviews.

chooseIf: You are an IT professional targeting a management role and want a NAAC A+ MBA with LinkedIn Learning tech certifications bundled.
Your budget is Rs 1,30,000 (opt-out) or Rs 1,50,000 with PAP deferred payment.
You want IT governance, cybersecurity, and product management content alongside your management core.
You value Pay After Placement for a career transition into IT management roles.
consider: You need PwC co-teaching and Harvard certificate for IT consulting or advisory roles at large firms (consider CU).
You need Amity's IT management brand specifically for large IT services company internal promotion criteria.
You want NAAC A++ and LPU's Punjab campus IT placement network (consider LPU).
